sections.html: NEXUS Security serves Texas businesses across multiple industries. Each client has unique challenges, regulatory requirements, and stakeholders. You’ll work with all four over the course of the semester.

sections.label: Brazos Financial Group — Modules 1 & 4
paragraphs: Dallas, Texas • Financial Services • 450 employees • $2.8B AUM

Wealth management firm serving high-net-worth clients across Texas and the Southwest. A recent merger with a smaller Austin firm brought legacy systems and compliance concerns. New SEC cybersecurity rules require formal security programs, and an examination is on the horizon. Leadership knows they have gaps but needs data to justify remediation budget.
Environment: Windows Active Directory   •   Key Issue: SEC compliance

sections.label: Gulf Coast Healthcare Partners — Modules 2 & 3
paragraphs: Regional healthcare system serving Greater Houston with acute care hospitals, specialty centers, and physician networks. A ransomware attack 18 months ago exposed significant security gaps. Since then they’ve invested heavily in security but need third-party validation. HIPAA compliance is non-negotiable — patient trust is everything.
Environment: Mixed (Linux servers, legacy Windows on medical devices)   •   Key Issue: HIPAA compliance, post-incident validation

sections.label: LoneStar DevOps — Modules 3 & 5
paragraphs: Fast-growing DevOps consulting firm seeking enterprise clients. Series A is closed; Series B depends on passing enterprise security reviews from prospective customers. A "move fast, break things" culture is colliding with enterprise compliance requirements. They need help fast and they’re willing to learn.
Environment: Cloud-native, containers, CI/CD pipelines   •   Key Issue: Enterprise security review readiness

sections.label: Alamo Industries — Module 4
paragraphs: Third-generation family manufacturing company with significant DoD contracts. CMMC Level 2 certification is required to keep federal business. Legacy OT systems haven’t seen serious updates in decades. Bobby Alamo III values stability and tradition, but the contracts won’t wait.
Environment: Mixed IT/OT, legacy systems, some Linux   •   Key Issue: CMMC Level 2 certification

sections.html: You’ll learn more about each client as their modules begin. Study your stakeholders — knowing who reads your report shapes how you write it.
